content.text:
{"id":"a51debb7-a3ba-4393-bf8f-bf08785858bb","identifier":"FRG-205","title":"Bug: No collision — characters walk through walls and non-walkable tiles","description":"Characters walk through furniture and objects. Root cause identified: buildWalkableGrid() marks the entire interior rectangle (cols 1-17, rows 2-9) as walkable. It does not exclude tiles occupied by objects.

## Object positions that should be non-walkable

* stove (3,3), fridge (2,3), tea_set (5,5), kotatsu (6,6), tv (9,3)
* bookshelf (15,3), desk (14,5), bed (16,5), easel (2,7), bonsai (11,8)

## Implementation

1. In buildWalkableGrid(), after building the base rectangle, mark object tiles as non-walkable
2. Read object positions from OBJECT_DEFS to build the exclusion list
3. This fix must be applied in BOTH debug.html and scene.js (they each have their own buildWalkableGrid)
4. Characters should interact with objects from an adjacent walkable tile (findAdjacentWalkable already handles this)

## Acceptance

* Characters never walk onto tiles occupied by objects
* Characters still path to adjacent tiles to interact with objects
* Both debug.html and scene.js use the same walkable grid logic
* Pathfinding still works (no unreachable areas created by blocking too many tiles)","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-205/bug-no-collision-characters-walk-through-walls-and-non-walkable-tiles","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-205-bug-no-collision-characters-walk-through-walls-and-non","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:01:08.933Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T04:04:37.100Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd","relations":{"blocks":[{"id":"cd797212-65b3-4590-ba49-6bb3e6346bf5","identifier":"FRG-209","title":"Create comprehensive QA test plan from M1 user stories"}],"blockedBy":[],"relatedTo":[],"duplicateOf":null}}

2. Root cause: `buildWalkableGrid()` marks the entire interior rectangle as walkable without excluding object positions
3. Fix needed in BOTH `debug.html` and `scene.js`
4. Object positions to mark non-walkable: stove (3,3), fridge (2,3), tea_set (5,5), kotatsu (6,6), tv (9,3), bookshelf (15,3), desk (14,5), bed (16,5), easel (2,7), bonsai (11,8)


5. Now I need to examine the codebase structure to see how the walkable grid is currently being built and where I need to make these changes.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. Both `debug.html` and `scene.js` have a `buildWalkableGrid()` function that marks all tiles in the rectangle (cols 1-17, rows 2-9) as walkable, but doesn't exclude tiles occupied by objects.

The fix needs to:
1. In `buildWalkableGrid()`, after building the base rectangle, mark object tiles as non-walkable
2. Read object positions from `OBJECT_DEFS` to build the exclusion list
3. Apply this fix in BOTH `debug.html` and `scene.js`

The object positions that should be non-walkable are:


- stove (3,3), fridge (2,3), tea_set (5,5), kotatsu (6,6), tv (9,3)
- bookshelf (15,3), desk (14,5), bed (16,5), easel (2,7), bonsai (11,8)

These are already defined in `OBJECT_DEFS` in `objects.js`. For `scene.js`, I need to import `OBJECT_DEFS` and iterate through it to mark those positions as non-walkable in the grid. For `debug.html`, `OBJECT_DEFS` is already imported, so I just need to apply the same logic there.

The fix involves updating `buildWalkableGrid()` in both files to loop through `OBJECT_DEFS` and set those coordinates as non-walkable.

Updating the function...

content.text: Now let me verify both changes look correct:

content.thinking: Both changes look correct. Let me also verify the import was added to `scene.js`:

content.text: Both changes are correct. Let me verify the import was added to `scene.js`:
Now let me also check the `Character.js` to understand how pathfinding uses the walkable grid — to confirm the fix will correctly prevent walking onto object tiles:

content.thinking:
Good. The `findPath` function checks `!walkable[n.y]?.[n.x]` for neighbors, so marking object tiles as `false` in the walkable grid will prevent pathfinding from routing through those tiles. Also note that `findPath` checks `!walkable[goalY]?.[goalX]` at the start — which means if we try to path to an object's tile directly, it will return `null`.

Now let me check the `Engine.js` to see how `findAdjacentWalkable` works — this is important since the issue says "Characters should interact with objects from an adjacent walkable tile (findAdjacentWalkable already handles this)".

content.text: Let me also check Engine.js to confirm `findAdjacentWalkable` is there and works correctly:

content.thinking:
Good. The `_findAdjacentWalkable` function looks at cardinally adjacent tiles and checks if they're walkable. Since we now mark object tiles as non-walkable, adjacent walkable tiles will be different from the object's tile itself. This is exactly what we need.

Note one concern: `_getWalkableTiles()` caches the walkable tiles lazily in `_walkableTiles`. Since `buildWalkableGrid()` returns the grid with object tiles as false, the `_getWalkableTiles()` will correctly build the list without object tiles.

Also, one important edge case: Ash's initial tile is (14, 5) which is the same as the desk object (14, 5). 

So Ash starts on a non-walkable tile—I need to verify the character definitions against the object positions to see if this is actually a problem or if there's something I'm missing about how initial placement works. I'm noticing a critical issue: Ash's starting position at (14, 5) is the exact same tile as the desk. If I mark that tile as non-walkable after the fix, the pathfinding algorithm will immediately fail when checking whether the start position is walkable, which breaks the core requirement that pathfinding still works. I need to move Ash's starting position away from the desk tile at (14, 5) since that will become non-walkable. Looking at the adjacent tiles, (15, 5) to the right of the desk should work as a valid starting position, or I could check (14, 4) above it depending on what other objects are nearby.

Actually, rereading the issue more carefully, it's specifically about fixing the pathfinding so characters don't walk through walls—the starting position might not be the core concern here. But if Ash starts on a tile that's marked as non-walkable, that could cause problems with the pathfinding system, so I should move her to an adjacent walkable tile to be safe.

Let me check if other character starting positions conflict with any object tiles.

Amelia at (6,7) and Lucy at (10,6) are both clear, but Ash at (14,5) overlaps with the desk, so I need to move Ash to (13,5) instead. Now I'm verifying that all adjacent tiles around each object are actually walkable to ensure pathfinding won't break. I'm continuing to verify that all adjacent tiles for the remaining furniture pieces—bookshelf, desk, bed, easel, and bonsai—fall within the valid walkable range and don't overlap with other objects.

Now I'm confirming that row 9 is indeed walkable since the interior spans rows 2-9, with the roof above and genkan below being non-walkable. Everything checks out, so I'll resolve the Ash position conflict by moving them from the desk location at (14, 5) to (13, 5) in both the scene and debug files.

content.text: I notice Ash's starting position (14, 5) is the same as the desk object tile (14, 5). After the fix, that tile becomes non-walkable, which would break Ash's pathfinding (`findPath` returns null if start is non-walkable). I need to move Ash's starting position to an adjacent walkable tile.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: mark object tiles non-walkable to prevent character collision (FRG-205)

- buildWalkableGrid() in scene.js and debug.html now reads OBJECT_DEFS
  and marks each object's tile as false after the base interior rectangle
  is built, so characters cannot walk onto furniture tiles
- Import OBJECT_DEFS into scene.js (was already imported in debug.html)
- Move Ash's spawn from (14,5) to (13,5) — desk occupies (14,5) which
  is now non-walkable, so spawning there would break A* pathfinding
- All 10 object tiles confirmed non-walkable; each has ≥3 adjacent
  walkable tiles so findAdjacentWalkable() still routes interactions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: mark object tiles non-walkable to prevent character collision (FRG-205)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- `buildWalkableGrid()` in both `scene.js` and `debug.html` now marks object-occupied tiles as non-walkable by iterating `OBJECT_DEFS` after building the base interior rectangle
- Added `import { OBJECT_DEFS }` to `scene.js` (debug.html already had it)
- Moved Ash's spawn from `(14,5)` to `(13,5)` — desk occupies `(14,5)` which is now non-walkable; A* returns null if start tile is non-walkable

## Verification
- All 10 object tiles confirmed `walkable=false`
- Every object has ≥3 adjacent walkable tiles, so `findAdjacentWalkable()` always finds a valid interaction approach tile
- All 3 character starting positions confirmed `walkable=true`

## Test plan
- [ ] Open `debug.html` and observe characters wander — they should not walk onto furniture tiles
- [ ] Characters should still walk adjacent to objects and trigger interactions
- [ ] Open `index.html` (Phaser scene) and verify same behaviour
- [ ] Pause/unpause, speed changes, and reset all function correctly

Closes FRG-205

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.content: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/3

input.body:
## Implementation complete

Fixed the collision bug by marking object tiles as non-walkable in `buildWalkableGrid()`.

**Files changed:**
- `src/scene.js` — added `import { OBJECT_DEFS }` and post-loop to set `grid[ty][tx] = false` for each object def
- `debug.html` — same post-loop added (OBJECT_DEFS was already imported)

**Key decision:** Ash's spawn was at `(14,5)` which is the desk tile. After the fix that tile becomes non-walkable, which would cause A* to return null immediately (it checks if start is walkable). Moved Ash's spawn to `(13,5)` — one tile to the left of the desk.

**Verified:**
- All 10 object tiles are non-walkable
- Every object has ≥3 adjacent walkable tiles so `findAdjacentWalkable()` always succeeds
- All 3 character starting positions are on walkable tiles

PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/3

Fixed the collision bug by marking object tiles as non-walkable in `buildWalkableGrid()`.

**Files changed:**
- `src/scene.js` — added `import { OBJECT_DEFS }` and post-loop to set `grid[ty][tx] = false` for each object def
- `debug.html` — same post-loop added (OBJECT_DEFS was already imported)

**Key decision:** Ash's spawn was at `(14,5)` which is the desk tile. After the fix that tile becomes non-walkable, which would cause A* to return null immediately (it checks if start is walkable). Moved Ash's spawn to `(13,5)` — one tile to the left of the desk.

content.text:
{"id":"a51debb7-a3ba-4393-bf8f-bf08785858bb","identifier":"FRG-205","title":"Bug: No collision — characters walk through walls and non-walkable tiles","description":"Characters walk through furniture and objects. Root cause identified: buildWalkableGrid() marks the entire interior rectangle (cols 1-17, rows 2-9) as walkable. It does not exclude tiles occupied by objects.

## Object positions that should be non-walkable

* stove (3,3), fridge (2,3), tea_set (5,5), kotatsu (6,6), tv (9,3)
* bookshelf (15,3), desk (14,5), bed (16,5), easel (2,7), bonsai (11,8)

## Implementation

1. In buildWalkableGrid(), after building the base rectangle, mark object tiles as non-walkable
2. Read object positions from OBJECT_DEFS to build the exclusion list
3. This fix must be applied in BOTH debug.html and scene.js (they each have their own buildWalkableGrid)
4. Characters should interact with objects from an adjacent walkable tile (findAdjacentWalkable already handles this)

## Acceptance

* Characters never walk onto tiles occupied by objects
* Characters still path to adjacent tiles to interact with objects
* Both debug.html and scene.js use the same walkable grid logic
* Pathfinding still works (no unreachable areas created by blocking too many tiles)","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-205/bug-no-collision-characters-walk-through-walls-and-non-walkable-tiles","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-205-bug-no-collision-characters-walk-through-walls-and-non","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:01:08.933Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T04:17:08.897Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-07T04:17:08.869Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"23d412a8-cfb2-47dc-9eb3-8b3256e45b7a","title":"fix: mark object tiles non-walkable to prevent character collision (FRG-205)","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/3"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Chill","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `src/scene.js` — imported `OBJECT_DEFS` from `objects.js`; added post-loop in `buildWalkableGrid()` to set `grid[ty][tx] = false` for each object def; moved Ash's spawn from `(14,5)` to `(13,5)`
- `debug.html` — added the same post-loop in `buildWalkableGrid()` (OBJECT_DEFS was already imported); moved Ash's spawn from `(14,5)` to `(13,5)`

## Key Decisions
- Ash's initial spawn `(14,5)` collides with the desk object. A* returns null if the start tile is non-walkable, so spawning there would permanently strand Ash. Moved spawn one tile left to `(13,5)`.
- Validated with Node.js: all 10 object tiles are `false` in the grid, every object has ≥3 adjacent walkable tiles, and all character spawns are on walkable tiles.

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/FRG-205-fix-walkable-grid-collision`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/3
